both of the wyed hoselines need to be considered.
only one of the wyed hoselines need to be considered.

questions.correctChoiceText: calculations are too difficult to make.

questions.question: When two hoselines of equal length are Siamesed to supply a fire stream, friction loss is approximately:

choices.text: the same as that of a single hoseline at the same nozzle pressure.
25 percent less than that of a single hoseline at the same nozzle pressure.
10 percent more than that of a single hoseline at the same nozzle pressure.
50 percent less than that of a single hoseline at the same nozzle pressure.

questions.correctChoiceText: 10 percent more than that of a single hoseline at the same nozzle pressure.

questions.question: Pressures for standpipe operations for fire departments may be:

choices.text: listed on the building entrance information.
posted on entry and exit passageways in the building.
labeled on the faceplate of the fire department connection.

questions.correctChoiceText: labeled on the faceplate of the fire department connection.

questions.question: To calculate friction loss when using multiple hoselines of unequal length, a driver/operator must:

choices.text: use estimates because calculations are too complex.
calculate friction loss for only one hoseline and then estimate the other.
calculate friction loss for the longest hoseline and double that number for the total.
calculate friction loss for each hoseline supplied by separate discharges to individual nozzles.

questions.correctChoiceText: use estimates because calculations are too complex.

questions.question: Which statement about adding unequal lengths of hose to an established wyed hose layout is MOST accurate?

choices.text: Friction loss must be determined for each attack line.
Friction loss can be assumed to be the same for all attack lines.
Friction loss only needs to be determined for the initial attack line.
Friction loss in a wyed hose layout does not need to be considered.

questions.correctChoiceText: Friction loss can be assumed to be the same for all attack lines.

questions.question: When determining pressure loss for elevated waterways, the elevation of the master stream:

choices.text: increases total pressure loss by 10 percent of height.
increases total pressure loss by 15 percent of height.
must be considered as part of the total pressure loss.
is not considered as it does not significantly affect pressure loss.

questions.correctChoiceText: increases total pressure loss by 10 percent of height.

questions.question: Pump discharge pressure must be sufficient to overcome:

choices.text: at least 75 percent of pressure loss.

questions.question: Which is the correct customary formula for determining pump discharge pressure?

choices.text: Nozzle pressure plus total pressure loss
Nozzle pressure divided by total pressure loss
Elevation loss plus friction loss minus nozzle pressure
Friction loss plus elevation loss multiplied by total pressure loss

questions.correctChoiceText: Nozzle pressure plus total pressure loss

questions.question: The pump discharge pressure should be calculated and set based on:

choices.text: average of all hoseline pressure requirements.
hoseline with the greatest pressure requirement.
median of lowest and highest pressure requirements.
hoseline most likely to be used more during operations.

questions.correctChoiceText: median of lowest and highest pressure requirements.

questions.question: For which of the following is a nozzle pressure of 50 psi (350 kPa) a safe and efficient nozzle pressure?
For which of the following is a nozzle pressure of 80 psi (560 kPa) a safe and efficient nozzle pressure?
For which of the following is a nozzle pressure of 100 psi (700 kPa) a safe and efficient nozzle pressure?
For which of the following is a nozzle pressure of 50 or 75 psi (350 or 525 kPa) a safe and efficient nozzle pressure?
When a pumper is being supplied by another pumper, net pump discharge pressure is the difference between pump discharge pressure and:

choices.text: average total pressure of both pumpers.
combined total pressure of both pumpers.

questions.correctChoiceText: average total pressure of both pumpers.

questions.question: Which is the correct formula for Net Pump Discharge Pressure?
The primary consideration for friction loss is the:

choices.text: volume of water flowing per minute.

questions.correctChoiceText: volume of water flowing per minute.

questions.question: Which BEST describes total pressure loss?

choices.text: Friction loss, elevation loss, and appliance loss
Friction loss and elevation loss, minus intake pressure
Friction loss and appliance loss multiplied by elevation loss

questions.correctChoiceText: Friction loss, elevation loss, and appliance loss

questions.question: Which statement about friction loss for handline nozzles, as part of the overall pressure loss of a hose assembly, is MOST accurate?

choices.text: 25 psi (175 kPa) loss is the maximum loss.
Loss is between 10 psi (70 kPa) and 25 psi (175 kPa).

questions.question: When using multiple hoselines of equal length but different diameter, calculations must be done for each hoseline, and the pump discharge pressure may then be set based on the:
What would be the FIRST step when determining friction loss in in Siamesed hoselines?

choices.text: Determine the friction loss in the Attack Line.
Determine the friction loss in the Supply Line.
Compute the total gallons per minute (L/min) flowing.
Determine the amount of friction loss in the Siamesed lines.

questions.correctChoiceText: Compute the total gallons per minute (L/min) flowing.

questions.question: When calculating pressure loss with a master stream device, the driver/operator must:

choices.text: add 10 psi (70 kPa) to the pressure loss calculation.
subtract 10 psi (70 kPa) to the pressure loss calculation.
add 25 psi (175 kPa) to the pressure loss calculation.
subtract 25 psi (175 kPa) to the pressure loss calculation.

questions.correctChoiceText: add 25 psi (175 kPa) to the pressure loss calculation.

questions.question: Which statement about pressure loss in elevated waterways is MOST accurate?

choices.text: Assume a pressure loss of 10 psi (70 kPa).
Each apparatus’ pressure loss will vary slightly.
Pressure loss is the same for various apparatus.
Elevation of the master stream is not considered.

questions.correctChoiceText: Each apparatus’ pressure loss will vary slightly.

questions.question: Which of the following are necessary to calculate pump discharge pressure?

choices.text: Length of hose lay and elevation
Fire stream type and friction loss
Nozzle pressure and sum of the total pressure loss

questions.correctChoiceText: Nozzle pressure and sum of the total pressure loss

questions.question: Flowmeters reflect water pressure that will be discharged through the nozzle:

choices.text: if the hoseline is functioning at its rated capacity.
if the hoseline is functioning at least 75% of its rated capacity.
after the hoseline has been operational for more than two to three minutes.

questions.question: Flowmeters are especially advantageous when automatic nozzles are employed with handlines or master stream devices because automatic nozzles may lead driver/operators to:

choices.text: allow the discharge pressure to vary excessively.
allow the discharge pressure to quickly overtake the water supply.
supply excessive discharge pressures, creating a high gallon (liter) per minute flow.
supply insufficient discharge pressures, creating a low gallon (liter) per minute flow.

questions.correctChoiceText: allow the discharge pressure to vary excessively.

questions.question: Which statement about the flowmeter is MOST accurate?

choices.text: It is generally accurate within 10 percent of actual water flow.
It is generally accurate within 25 percent of actual water flow.
It serves as a check to verify calculations based on friction loss, elevation pressure, and length of hoseline.
It relieves you from relying on calculations based on friction loss, elevation pressure, and length of hoseline.

questions.correctChoiceText: It relieves you from relying on calculations based on friction loss, elevation pressure, and length of hoseline.

questions.question: Which type of flowmeter is mounted at the top of a straight section of discharge pipe so that only a small portion of the device extends into the waterway?
Which type of flowmeter uses a sensor to measure the speed at which it spins and translates the information into a flow measurement?
Which type of flowmeter employs a stainless steel spring probe to detect water movement in discharge piping?
Which type of flowmeter has only one simple moving part and tends to require little maintenance?
A flowmeter should be accurate to a tolerance of plus or minus:
Each discharge equipped with a flowmeter must have a digital readout display mounted within:

choices.text: 2 inches (50 mm) of the control valve for that discharge.
6 inches (150 mm) of the control valve for that discharge.
8 inches (200 mm) of the control valve for that discharge.
12 inches (300 mm) of the control valve for that discharge.

questions.correctChoiceText: 8 inches (200 mm) of the control valve for that discharge.

questions.question: Which is MOST likely to be the cause if a driver/operator receives a message from an attack team that the volume of water at the nozzle has suddenly decreased without a corresponding change at the flowmeter?

choices.text: The water supply has been compromised.
An object has fallen on top of the hoseline.
The attack team is improperly using appliances.

questions.correctChoiceId:

Flow through any specific discharge
Total water flow through pump in “real time”
Total water flowed through pump for duration of incident
Amount of foam being flowed

choices.text: A volume of water flowing from a receiving pumper can be increased substantially.
A receiving pumper can refill at a much quicker pace and the driver/operator can focus on tasks.
All pumpers in the operation can refill with the use of fewer personnel.

questions.correctChoiceId:

Manual: Consists of a slide or dial that can be moved to cover or reveal rates of flow, size of hose, and length of hose layout in order to come to a conclusion to a problem
Electronic: Specially programmed to allow driver/operator to input variables of each hose layout, calculator then applies preprogrammed formulas to arrive at pump discharge pressure

questions.question: When a line from a standpipe operation is shut down, the pressure:

choices.text: must be entirely shut down and the system restarted.
must be increased, or the standpipe system will not flow enough water through remaining hoselines.
must be lowered accordingly, or the standpipe system will attempt to flow excess water through remaining hoselines.
can remain the same, as the standpipe system will automatically adjust and the remaining hoselines will flow correctly.

questions.correctChoiceId:
Answers may vary; students should include points from the following:
Locate the nozzle or the layout in question on the chart
Account for the number of feet (meters) in the layout
Plot where each column intersects to find the required pump discharge pressure

questions.question: The driver/operator must consider that when a standpipe or hoseline is charged, there:

choices.text: may be residual pressure that will cause flow before nozzle is opened.
is no flow through the system or hose layout until the nozzle is opened.
will be a significant delay from the time the nozzle is opened until full flow.
can be significant flow through the system even when the nozzle is unopened.

questions.question: Which statement about manual hydraulic calculators is MOST accurate?

choices.text: Consists of a graph in which the intersections of variables provide pump discharge pressure
Consists of multiple charts in which the user finds rates of flow, size of hose, and length of hose layout
Consists of index cards that are cross-referenced to rates of flow, size of hose, and length of hose layout
Consists of a slide or dial that can be moved to cover or reveal rates of flow, size of hose, and length of hose layout

questions.question: Which statement about electronic hydraulic calculators is MOST accurate?

choices.text: Once pump discharge pressure is determined, it must also be manually verified.
After variables have been entered, the user must enter the correct formula to arrive at pump discharge pressure.
Once variables have been entered, the calculator applies preprogrammed formulas to arrive at pump discharge pressure.
Once variables have been entered, the correct formula is shown and the user must manually arrive at pump discharge pressure.

questions.question: Electronic hydraulic calculators may be handheld or may be:

choices.text: permanently affixed to the inside of the driver’s door.
integrated into the mobile data communications device.
permanently affixed to a location near the pump operator’s panel.
located at the station and calculations are provided over the radio as they are needed.

questions.correctChoiceText: integrated into the mobile data communications device.

questions.question: Which statement about pump charts is MOST accurate?

choices.text: They contain information that must be updated at least monthly for accuracy.
They contain guidelines for determining pump discharge pressure, but not actual numbers.

They contain generic information regarding the most common hose layouts used by departments.

questions.correctChoiceText: They contain generic information regarding the most common hose layouts used by departments.

questions.question: When developing a customized pump chart, column headings should include information such as flow, nozzle pressure, and:

choices.text: distance in feet for hose layouts.

questions.correctChoiceText: distance in feet for hose layouts.

questions.question: Which is a guideline for initial calculations when developing a customized pump chart?

choices.text: Develop a main scenario and use it as a basis.
Include friction loss for all handline appliances.
Include friction loss for master stream appliances.
Pump discharge pressure can exceed test pressure for calculations.

questions.correctChoiceText: Include friction loss for all handline appliances.

questions.question: A guideline when developing a customized pump chart is to:

choices.text: round pump discharge pressure calculations to the nearest 5 psi (35 kPa).
round pump discharge pressure calculations to the nearest 10 psi (70 kPa).
round pump discharge pressure calculations to the nearest 20 psi (140 kPa).

questions.correctChoiceText: round pump discharge pressure calculations to the nearest 10 psi (70 kPa).

questions.question: A driver/operator uses a pump chart by locating the nozzle or the layout in question on the chart, accounting for the number of feet (meters) in the layout, and:

choices.text: reading across the row to find the required pump discharge pressure.
multiplying the number of feet by the diameter of the hose used in layout.
plotting where each column intersects to find the required pump discharge pressure.
inserting the required numbers into an electronic calculator to come up with correct pump discharge pressure.

questions.correctChoiceText: inserting the required numbers into an electronic calculator to come up with correct pump discharge pressure.

questions.question: The condensed Q formula may be used for operations in which the friction loss can be determined for:

choices.text: It is only applicable to fog streams.

choices.text: for 3-inch hose manufactured within the past 5 years.

the driver/operator to perform additional calculations.
that the driver/operator input variables into a calculator.

choices.text: is functioning at its rated capacity.

is functioning within 25% of its rated capacity.

questions.question: Which type of flowmeter has only one simple moving part and therefore tends to require little maintenance?

choices.text: Will have no effect on the flowmeter’s reading
Will cause the flowmeter to immediately shut down
Will cause the flowmeter to malfunction, giving incorrect readings

questions.question: When a line is shut down during standpipe operations and the driver/operator is operating an apparatus equipped with a flowmeter, the driver/operator should:

choices.text: monitor the system for excess flow.

questions.question: A guideline when making initial calculations for a customized pump chart is to:

choices.text: exceed maximum discharge pressure by 10% to account for errors.
disregard residual pressure at the intake of the pumper being supplied.
ensure the length of the wyed hoseline layout is the distance between the pumper and the wye.

choices.text: The formula cannot be used with metric measurements.
The method may be used for operations with any size hose.

questions.question: Which statement about positive displacement pumps is MOST accurate?

choices.text: They are the main pumping unit on fire apparatus.
They are no longer useful in the modern fire service.

questions.correctChoiceText: They are no longer useful in the modern fire service.

Which type of positive displacement pump is the simplest of all fire apparatus pumps, from the standpoint of design?

toward the inside edges of the disk.
in a very tight circle around the center of the disk.
intermittently toward the outer and inner edges of the disk.

questions.correctChoiceText: toward the inside edges of the disk.

the harder water is thrown, giving the water more velocity.

choices.text: A centrifugal pump can pump air and is self priming.

choices.text: have no effect on the pump.

questions.correctChoiceText: are identical and have the same capacity.

choices.text: each impeller must flow 100 percent of rated capacity.
only the first impeller takes water from a source and delivers it to the discharge.
each of the impellers takes water from a source and delivers it to the discharge.

questions.correctChoiceText: only the first impeller takes water from a source and delivers it to the discharge.

questions.question: When pumping in the series (pressure) position:

choices.text: water is only directed into the second impeller.
only the first impeller takes water from a source and delivers it to the discharge.
each of the impellers takes water from a source and delivers it to the discharge.
all water from the intake manifold is directed into the eye of the first impeller.

a much higher pressure than would be achieved in parallel operation.

choices.text: has little immediate effect on the discharge pressure.

questions.correctChoiceId:
Answers should include the following.

ensure the pump is entirely full of water at all times.

questions.correctChoiceText: It is acceptable for the seals to run waterless, as no damage will be incurred.

choices.text: Maintain a high level of personal physical fitness
Create a physical training program for new recruits
Purchase the newest available personal protective equipment
Utilize the fire department’s member assistance program resources

questions.correctChoiceText: Purchase the newest available personal protective equipment

questions.question: What is the leading cause of firefighter fatalities?

choices.text: decrease the toxic gases and vapors in smoke.
reduce the amount of chemicals deposited on protective gear.
increase the skin’s ability to absorb chemicals deposited on it.
lessen the likelihood of firefighters being contaminated in most fires.

questions.correctChoiceText: reduce the amount of chemicals deposited on protective gear.

choices.text: work downwind of fires and smoke plumes.

questions.correctChoiceText: clean contaminated turnouts at least once a month.

choices.text: increased occurrences of illnesses and diseases.

questions.correctChoiceText: decreased chances of developing acute or chronic illnesses.

choices.text: cause cancer, heart disease, and COPD.

questions.question: Which statement about atypically stressful events is MOST accurate?

choices.text: Individuals should be able to cope with most atypically stressful events on their own.
CISD remains the most effective means of treating individuals following an atypical stressful event.

questions.question: What is a function of station and work uniforms?

choices.text: Provide protection against advanced fire behavior
Identify wearer as a member of the fire department

choices.text: clothing made of non-fire-resistant synthetic materials.

choices.text: Protect from temperatures of up to 1,000°F (540°C)
Prevent burns from extreme fire behaviors such as flashover
Cover all portions of skin while reaching, bending, or moving

questions.correctChoiceText: lay flat beneath the wristlets of the protective coat.

choices.text: Never share PPE with another firefighter.
Always wear protective clothing that fits properly.

Make sure there is no overlap between the coat and trousers.

questions.correctChoiceText: Always wear the protective gloves beneath the coat wristlets.

questions.question: Which statement about inspecting PPE is MOST accurate?

choices.text: PPE must be inspected at the start of every shift and after every use.

choices.text: It should be removed from service and destroyed.

questions.question: How might a Fire Fighter I exhibit situational awareness in action on the fireground?  (A-49-50)

Following an order from the Incident Commander to advance a hoseline

questions.question: Which statement regarding apparatus safety is MOST accurate?

choices.text: When exiting the cab, backing out can cause a serious tripping hazard.
Passengers must wear fire fighting helmets while in the apparatus cab.
The safest location to mount and dismount apparatus is on the side of the vehicle exposed to traffic.
Firefighters should always maintain three points of contact when mounting or dismounting apparatus.

Save lives and protect property and the environment from fires and other hazardous situations
